$comment: Paste these into the Vapi dashboard → Tools tab. The serverUrl values point at the Vercel production deployment of ringdesk.co. async=false because the AI must wait for the response before continuing the conversation. After saving each tool, attach both to the assistant 53ca6567-707b-46bf-bfd7-cc80abcb9fb7.

function.name: check_availability
function.description: Use when the caller wants to schedule a discovery call or demo with Tony at RingDesk. Returns up to 8 available 15-minute slots over the next 7 days. Each slot has a `spoken` field you can read aloud verbatim (e.g. 'Tomorrow at 9 AM Mountain'). Only invoke this for prospects who are interested in RingDesk's service — never for existing customers calling about their account, vendors, or wrong numbers. After receiving slots, do NOT list all 8 — offer 2 or 3 and ask the caller if any of those work, then offer the next batch if needed.

daysAhead.description: How many days ahead to check (default 7, max 14). Leave default unless the caller asks for a specific date range.

function.name: book_appointment
function.description: Use after the caller has chosen a specific slot from check_availability AND you have collected their name, business name, and phone number (email is optional — pass null if they refuse). Books the 15-minute discovery call on Tony's Cal.com calendar. If the response indicates SLOT_TAKEN, apologize, immediately call check_availability again, and offer the next available slot. On success, read the confirmation script: 'Great — I've got you booked for [confirmedTimeSpoken]. You'll get a confirmation email and text shortly.'

startTimeIso.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the chosen slot — copy the `iso` field from the slot the caller picked in check_availability. Do NOT improvise or modify this value.

name.description: Caller's full name (first and last if given).

email.description: Caller's email address. Pass null if the caller declines to give one — we'll synthesize a placeholder.

phone.description: Caller's phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +19705551234). If the caller gives it in another format, normalize to E.164.

businessName.description: The caller's business name (the trades business they own or work for).

reason.description: Optional — one short sentence on why they want to talk (e.g. 'wants pricing for a 2-tech plumbing company in Loveland'). Pass empty string if the caller didn't say.
